title: Switzerland on a Budget — 5 Days
subtitle: Free hikes, cheap eats, and smart savings across Switzerland
description: Switzerland doesn't have to break the bank. This budget-conscious itinerary focuses on free activities, affordable transport hacks, and incredible experiences that don't cost a fortune.

itinerary.title: Bern — The Free Capital
itinerary.description: Bern's entire old town is a UNESCO site — and free to explore. See the Zytglogge clock, the bear park, swim in the Aare river (free!), and visit the free Einstein Museum exhibit.
itinerary.tip: Bern's museums are free on the first Saturday of each month

itinerary.title: Hiking the Swiss Alps (Free)
itinerary.description: Take the gondola to Oeschinensee and hike around the lake. Pack a picnic lunch. The scenery rivals any paid experience in Switzerland.
itinerary.tip: Gondola is CHF 24 return — the rest is free

itinerary.title: Lucerne on a Budget
itinerary.description: Walk the free Chapel Bridge and old town, hike up to Château Gütsch for free panoramic views, and swim in the lake. Evening Saver Day Pass trip back.
itinerary.transport: Use Saver Day Pass (from CHF 52) for this day

itinerary.title: Swiss National Park (Free)
itinerary.description: Switzerland's only national park is free to enter. Hike through pristine wilderness, spot ibex and marmots, and enjoy some of the cleanest air in Europe.

itinerary.title: Zurich Free Day & Departure
itinerary.description: Free walking tour of Zurich's old town, Lindenhof viewpoint, and a swim at Badi Utoquai (free lake swim). Browse the flea market at Bürkliplatz.
itinerary.tip: Zurich's public lake baths (Badi) are free or under CHF 8
